meta.name: Minestrone
meta.subtitle: Pantry soup with beans + pasta • highly adaptable, hearty, and freezer-friendly (with notes)

source.notes: SSA baseline: pantry-forward minestrone built for 'use what you have' logic. Pasta can be cooked in-soup or separately for best leftovers.

yield.notes: Makes ~14–16 cups depending on vegetable volume and simmer.

tasteProfile.notes: Tomato-forward, herbaceous, hearty with beans and tender vegetables; finish with greens and parmesan (optional).

ingredients.label: Italian seasoning (or a blend of oregano + basil + thyme)
Vegetable broth (or water + bouillon), low-sodium preferred
ingredients.notes: About 7 cups. Start with 6 cups and add more as needed.

ingredients.label: Beans, cooked/drained (cannellini, kidney, or chickpeas)
ingredients.notes: About 3 cups cooked OR two 15-oz cans drained/rinsed. Mix types if desired.

ingredients.label: Zucchini, diced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Add later so it doesn't turn mushy.

ingredients.label: Potato, diced (optional, for extra heartiness)
ingredients.notes: Add early so it cooks through.

ingredients.label: Small pasta (ditalini, elbows, small shells)
ingredients.notes: About 1 cup dry. See planner notes for cooking separately.

ingredients.label: Leafy greens (kale, spinach, chard), chopped
ingredients.notes: About 4 cups loosely packed. Add at the end.

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Start with ~1 1/4 teaspoons; adjust at end.

ingredients.label: Parmesan, grated (optional topping)
ingredients.notes: Allergen: dairy. For vegan, omit or use nutritional yeast.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice or red wine vinegar (optional, brightness)

steps.title: Sweat aromatics
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook 8–10 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Bloom tomato paste and herbs (optional but recommended)
instructions: If using tomato paste, stir it into the vegetables and cook 1–2 minutes until it darkens slightly and smells sweet.
Stir in Italian seasoning (and red pepper flakes if using) for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Build the broth and simmer vegetables
instructions: Add crushed tomatoes, broth (start with ~6 cups), bay leaf (optional), salt, and pepper.
If using potato, add it now.
Bring to a gentle simmer and cook 15 minutes.

steps.title: Add beans and sturdier vegetables
instructions: Stir in beans and green beans (if using).
Simmer 10 minutes to meld flavors and soften vegetables.

steps.title: Cook pasta (in-soup or separately)
instructions: Option A (simple): stir pasta directly into the soup and simmer until tender (8–10 minutes). Add extra broth/water as needed.
Option B (best leftovers): cook pasta separately in salted water, then add to each bowl when serving (prevents pasta from soaking up all broth).

steps.title: Finish with quick-cooking vegetables and greens
instructions: If using zucchini or corn, add now and simmer 3–5 minutes.
Stir in greens and cook 1–3 minutes until wilted.

steps.title: Season and serve
instructions: Add a small squeeze of lemon juice or a splash of vinegar (optional) to brighten.

beanOptions: Cannellini (creamy), kidney (classic), chickpeas (firm). Mix types freely.
No beans? Use lentils or add diced potato/sweet potato for heartiness.
vegetableSwaps: Swap in whatever you have: cabbage, peas, mushrooms, squash, carrots, spinach, kale, frozen mixed vegetables.
Add tender greens at the end; add hard vegetables earlier.
pastaNotes: For meal-prep, cook pasta separately to preserve brothiness over multiple days.
Gluten-free pasta works; add near the end and watch cooking time closely.

bestPractices: For freezer: freeze soup WITHOUT pasta for best texture. Add freshly cooked pasta when reheating.
Cool quickly; portion into containers or flat bags for fast thaw.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 5 days. Soup thickens; add broth/water on reheat.
storage.freezer: Freeze up to 3 months (best without pasta).
storage.reheat: Reheat gently to a simmer. Add greens fresh if you prefer brighter texture.

notes: This is intentionally flexible. The base pattern is: aromatics + tomato + broth + beans + vegetables + starch + greens.
If you want a richer, non-vegetarian version: use chicken broth and add diced cooked chicken or sausage at the end (keep vegetarian flag off if you do).

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions; no disallowed top-level properties.
